24.reason: [Using bitwise NOT on fscanf return is non-portable and may cause infinite loop; missing width specifier may cause buffer overflow; also, tokenizing by whitespace does not split on punctuation, leading to incorrect word extraction.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using bitwise NOT on fscanf return is non-portable and may cause infinite loop; missing width specifier may cause buffer overflow; also, tokenizing by whitespace does not split on punctuation, leading to incorrect word extraction.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Using read without checking if it is NULL; if file does not exist, this leads to segmentation fault or undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The bitwise NOT operator ~ applied to the return value of fscanf is non-standard and may cause compilation errors in strict environments. It should be replaced with fscanf(...) != EOF.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Using ~fscanf is unsafe; fscanf can return 0 on match failure, causing an infinite loop. Should use while (fscanf(...) == 1).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using bitwise NOT (~) on fscanf return value is incorrect; fscanf returns EOF on failure, which is typically -1, and ~(-1) is 0, causing loop to terminate immediately or behave unpredictably. Should use != EOF instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using bitwise NOT (~) on fscanf return value is incorrect; fscanf returns EOF on failure, which is -1, and ~(-1) is 0, causing loop to exit immediately. Should use != EOF or > 0 instead.]

15.reason: No check for fopen failure; if file missing, read is NULL, causing undefined behavior in subsequent fscanf and fclose.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  No check for fopen failure; if file missing, read is NULL, causing undefined behavior in subsequent fscanf and fclose.

82.reason: If fopen failed, read is NULL, and fclose(NULL) is undefined behavior.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If fopen failed, read is NULL, and fclose(NULL) is undefined behavior.

26.reason: [fscanf with %s skips whitespace, so arr will never contain "\n". This condition is logically incorrect and should be removed.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [fscanf with %s skips whitespace, so arr will never contain "\n". This condition is logically incorrect and should be removed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  fscanf with %s skips whitespace, so arr will never be "\n". This check is logically incorrect and redundant.

28.reason: Buffer arr[32] may overflow if a word exceeds 31 characters. Also, calling strlen in loop condition is inefficient.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Buffer arr[32] may overflow if a word exceeds 31 characters. Also, calling strlen in loop condition is inefficient.

33.reason: Same buffer overflow risk and inefficiency as above.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Same buffer overflow risk and inefficiency as above.
